metric: 2.1.1 Game Lobby Module: Main Menu display and integrity
description:
1. **Act:** Run the main program.
2. **Assert:** After launching, observe whether the command line immediately shows a clear main menu, and the menu must fully include these five options: "Start New Match", "Continue Match", "View Historical Data", "Data Analysis Center", and "Exit System".
expected_output: Main menu is displayed completely, including the five options: "Start New Match", "Continue Match", "View Historical Data", "Data Analysis Center", and "Exit System".

metric: 2.1.2a Game Lobby Module: Menu Input Validation – Reject Invalid Input
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the main program. b) Try entering each of the following: a number out of the option range (such as '9'), a non-numeric character (such as 'a'), and a special symbol (such as '$').
2. **Assert:** After each invalid input, observe whether the system rejects the operation and stably returns to the main menu interface waiting for the next input.
expected_output: For invalid inputs '9', 'a', '$', an error message is shown and the user is returned to the main menu.

metric: 2.1.2b Game Lobby Module: Menu Input Validation – Chinese Error Prompt
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the main program. b) Enter an invalid option, such as 'a'.
2. **Assert:** Check whether the error message returned by the system is clear and in proper Chinese with no garbled characters.
expected_output: Includes an error prompt in Chinese, such as "Invalid input, please enter a number".

metric: 2.1.3 System Configuration Module: Function Accessibility
description:
1. **Act:** Run the main program, check the main menu or other top-level system menus.
2. **Assert:** There should be a clear and independent entry point (such as a menu item "System Configuration" or "Settings") to access the three functions: "Player Info Management", "Chessboard Display Style Switch", and "Clear Data".
expected_output: After entering the system configuration menu, options for "Player Info Management", "Chessboard Display Style Switch", and "Clear Data" are displayed.

metric: 2.1.4a System Configuration Module: Create Player Profile
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program and navigate to the "System Configuration" menu to select the "Clear Data" feature. b) Go to the "Player Info Management" interface, select to create a new player, and enter the ID "Player_Create_Test". c) After creation, select the "View All Players" feature.
2. **Assert:** a) Check whether the system indicates successful creation. b) In the "View All Players" list, you can see the name "Player_Create_Test".
expected_output: System indicates success, and 'Player_Create_Test' appears in the player list.

metric: 2.1.4b System Configuration Module: Delete Player Profile
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, first use the "Player Info Management" function to create a new player with ID "Player_ToDelete". b) Enter the "Player Info Management" interface again, select the delete player feature, and specify to delete "Player_ToDelete". c) After deletion, select the "View All Players" feature again.
2. **Assert:** a) Check whether the system indicates successful deletion. b) In the "View All Players" list, the record for player "Player_ToDelete" is removed.
expected_output: System indicates successful deletion, and 'Player_ToDelete' can no longer be found in the player list.

metric: 2.1.5 System Configuration Module: Maximum Number of Player Profiles
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, navigate to the "System Configuration" menu, select the "Clear Data" feature. b) Use the "Player Info Management" function to continuously create 10 new players. c) Try to create the 11th player through the player creation feature.
2. **Assert:** Check whether the system clearly indicates "limit reached" or similar information, and refuses to add.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming that the 11th player cannot be added.

metric: 2.1.6 System Configuration Module: Chessboard Display Style Switch
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, navigate to the "Chessboard Display Style Switch" feature, and select "Compact" mode. b) Start a new game. c) Exit the match, then go to style switch again and select "Expanded" mode. d) Start another new game.
2. **Assert:** a) The chessboard displayed when starting the second step should use the compact style. b) The chessboard in the fourth step uses the expanded style. The switch should not require restarting the program.
expected_output: First game displays compact chessboard, second game displays expanded chessboard.

metric: 2.2.1 Gomoku Module: Start Match & Board Initialization
description:
1. **Act:** Run the program, select "Start New Match" from main menu, then choose "Gomoku".
2. **Assert:** System successfully enters the match interface and renders a 15x15 initial chessboard, all cells marked with '+' and no moves played yet.
expected_output: Successfully renders a 15x15 chessboard with '+' as the initial symbol.

metric: 2.2.2a Gomoku Module: Move Validity – Accept Legal Coordinates
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Gomoku match. b) When it is black's ('●') turn, enter a legal coordinate within the board, such as "H8".
2. **Assert:** The board correctly shows '●' at H8, and the game indicates it is white's turn.
expected_output: '●' is shown at H8, and it is white's turn.

metric: 2.2.2b Gomoku Module: Move Validity – Reject Out-of-Bounds Coordinates
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Gomoku match. b) Enter an out-of-bounds coordinate, such as "P16" or "A16".
2. **Assert:** System rejects the move, gives an error prompt, and asks the current player to re-enter.
expected_output: System gives an illegal move prompt for both 'P16' and 'A16'.

metric: 2.2.2c Gomoku Module: Move Validity – Reject Duplicate Moves
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Gomoku match. b) The first player moves at H8. c) The next player tries to move at H8 again.
2. **Assert:** System rejects the move, gives an error prompt, and asks the current player to re-enter.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming that duplicate moves on the same spot are not allowed.

metric: 2.2.3 Gomoku Module: Winning Condition Determination
description:
1. **Act:** For horizontal, vertical, diagonal, and anti-diagonal directions, do the following: a) Start a new match. b) Through moves, construct a "one move away from victory" situation (for horizontal, e.g., black moves at A1,B1,C1,D1, white at A2,B2,C2,D2). c) Play the decisive move (e.g., move at E1).
2. **Assert:** After a five-in-a-row is formed, the system should instantly judge the result, announce the winner, and end the match. All four directions must pass.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming all four five-in-a-row win scenarios are correctly detected.

metric: 2.2.4a Gomoku Module: Match Operation – Undo Move
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new match. b) First player moves at H8. c) Second player moves at H9. d) When the first player’s turn, enter the "undo" command. e) After successful undo, immediately enter the "undo" command again.
2. **Assert:** a) After the first undo, the board should be restored to before the second player’s move (H9 is empty). b) System indicates "cannot undo consecutively" or similar, and rejects the second undo.
expected_output: First undo is successful and the board is restored. Second undo is rejected with a prompt such as "cannot undo consecutively".

metric: 2.2.4b Gomoku Module: Match Operation – Surrender
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Gomoku match. b) During any player's turn, enter the "surrender" command.
2. **Assert:** The match immediately ends and the other side is correctly announced as the winner.
expected_output: Match ends and the opponent is announced as the winner.

metric: 2.3.1 Chinese Chess Module: Initial Board and Piece Layout
description:
1. **Act:** Run the program, select "Start New Match" from the main menu, then choose "Chinese Chess".
2. **Assert:** The board is 9x10, contains the "Chu River Han Border" marking, and all 32 pieces are in their correct initial positions using the correct character symbols.
expected_output: Successfully renders a 9x10 Chinese Chess board, with Chu River Han Border and all pieces correctly placed.

metric: 2.3.2a Chinese Chess Module: Core Move – Horse
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Chinese Chess match. b) Move the "horse" via a clear "日" (L-shaped) path. c) Place a piece at the horse's turning point (hindering the move), and try to move the horse past that spot again.
2. **Assert:** a) First move is successful. b) Second move is judged as illegal and is prohibited by the system.
expected_output: Test passes, both the horse's "L shape" move and the hindering leg rule are correctly applied.

metric: 2.3.2b Chinese Chess Module: Core Move – Elephant
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Chinese Chess match. b) Move the "elephant" across an unobstructed "田" square. c) Place a piece in the center of the "田" square (blocking the move), and try to move the elephant past that spot again.
2. **Assert:** a) First move is successful. b) Second move is judged as illegal and is prohibited by the system.
expected_output: Test passes, both the elephant's "square" move and the blocking rule are correctly applied.

metric: 2.3.2c Chinese Chess Module: Core Move – Cannon
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new Chinese Chess match. b) Move the "cannon" to an empty spot along a path with no pieces. c) Place a piece between the cannon and an opponent's piece (acting as the cannon platform), then move the cannon to capture the opponent's piece.
2. **Assert:** a) First move is successful. b) Second move captures successfully.
expected_output: Test passes, both the cannon's move and "cannon across the mountain" rule are correctly handled.

metric: 2.3.3 Chinese Chess Module: Special Rule – Generals Facing
description:
1. **Act:** a) Start a new match. b) Through moves, set up a situation where the "general" and "marshal" are on the same straight line with just one piece in between. c) Try moving that last blocking piece.
2. **Assert:** The system judges such move as illegal and prohibits it.
expected_output: Test passes, system can correctly prevent moves that would cause generals directly facing.

metric: 2.4.1a Match Data Recording Module: Record Generation
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, go to "System Configuration" menu and select "Clear Data". b) Start a new match, set match ID to "TestGame01", complete the match and finish normally. c) Return to the main menu and select "View Historical Data".
2. **Assert:** In the history data list, you can see the match record with ID "TestGame01".
expected_output: After match ends, the corresponding record is shown in the "View Historical Data" menu.

metric: 2.4.1b Match Data Recording Module: Record Integrity
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, go to "System Configuration" menu and select "Clear Data", and finish a match. b) Return to the main menu and select "View Historical Data".
2. **Assert:** In the history data list, the latest match record shows exact match of key information such as game type, players, and results with the just completed match.
expected_output: Core data shown in history record is accurate and matches the match.

metric: 2.4.2 Match Data Recording Module: Data Recovery After Abnormal Exit
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, start a new game and play 6 moves. b) Force-close the program. c) Restart and select "Continue Match" on the main menu.
2. **Assert:** Observe whether the recovered game resumes from step 5 (or nearby, since every 5 steps are cached).
test_input:
python src/main.py
5
3
yes

1
1
PlayerA

1
PlayerB

3
4
1
1
1
2
H8
A1
H7
A2
H6
A3
^C
python src/main.py
evaluation/2.4.2_recovery.in
expected_output: After re-entering the game, the state is restored to step 5.

metric: 2.5.1 Data Analysis Center: Function Accessibility and Main Interface
description:
1. **Act:** Run the program, select "Data Analysis Center" in the main menu.
2. **Assert:** Successfully enter a new menu, and the menu clearly lists at least three analysis features plus a data export option as defined in the PRD.
expected_output: Successfully enter the Data Analysis Center, displaying all analysis options.

metric: 2.5.2 Data Analysis Center: 7-Day Match Frequency Ranking
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, use the "Clear Data" feature. b) Create players PlayerA, PlayerB, PlayerC. c) Play 3 matches with PlayerA, 2 with PlayerB, and 1 with PlayerC, ensuring all match dates are within the past 7 days. d) Navigate to "Data Analysis Center" and perform the "7-Day Match Frequency Ranking" feature.
2. **Assert:** Output result should be a list ranked strictly as PlayerA > PlayerB > PlayerC.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming player ranking is correctly sorted by match frequency.

metric: 2.5.3a Data Analysis Center: Win Rate Statistics (Basic)
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, use "Clear Data". b) Create PlayerA. c) Use PlayerA for two Gomoku matches (one win, one loss) and one Chinese Chess match (one win). d) Navigate to "Win Rate Statistics" in Data Analysis Center and calculate total win rate for PlayerA.
2. **Assert:** PlayerA's total win rate is displayed as (2/3)*100% ≈ 66.7%.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming the total win rate for PlayerA is calculated correctly.

metric: 2.5.3b Data Analysis Center: Win Rate Statistics (Filtered)
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, use "Clear Data". b) Create PlayerA. c) Use PlayerA for two Gomoku matches (one win, one loss) and one Chinese Chess match (one win). d) Navigate to "Win Rate Statistics" in Data Analysis Center and view PlayerA's win rate filtered by "Gomoku" and "Chinese Chess".
2. **Assert:** After filtering, Gomoku win rate is 50%, Chinese Chess win rate is 100%.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming win rates filtered by game type are calculated correctly.

metric: 2.5.4 Data Analysis Center: Opening Move Heatmap Visualization
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, use "Clear Data". b) Play at least three Gomoku matches and ensure the first move is at "H8" in each. c) Go to Data Analysis Center and generate the Gomoku "Opening Move Heatmap".
2. **Assert:** In the output ASCII heatmap, the hottest (or highest value) point is clearly marked at H8.
expected_output: Test passes, confirming that opening move heatmap data is correctly counted.

metric: 2.5.5a Data Analysis Center: Data Export (File Generation)
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, complete at least one match to generate data. b) Navigate to any analysis function and successfully generate results. c) Select "Export Data" feature and specify filename as `export.csv`.
2. **Assert:** Check if `export.csv` file is generated successfully in the working directory.
expected_output: `export.csv` file is successfully generated in the working directory.

metric: 2.5.5b Data Analysis Center: Data Export (Header and Content)
description:
1. **Act:** a) Run the program, complete at least one match to generate data. b) Navigate to any analysis function and successfully generate results. c) Select "Export Data" function and successfully generate `export.csv`. d) Open the `export.csv` file.
2. **Assert:** Check whether the first line of the file is a clear, meaningful field header and whether the file contents match the analysis results displayed on screen.
expected_output: Content of the generated export.csv matches evaluation/expected_export.csv.
